title: phaseolus vulgaris var. alboruber
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. alboruber, commonly known as the white kidney bean, is a variant of the common bean plant (Phaseolus vulgaris) that is native to Central and South America. This plant contains a unique compound called alpha-amylase inhibitor, which has been found to have medicinal properties that can help inhibit the digestion of carbohydrates, particularly starches, which can aid in weight loss and glucose management. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. alboruber include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve insulin sensitivity, and support weight loss efforts, making it a popular ingredient in herbal weight loss supplements. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into capsules, tablets, or powders, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
